pages.notes: Page contains a hand-drawn flat-pattern/template diagram for a box, labelled as a 'cake box from Cluny'. Labels within the diagram include 'x 2', what appears to be 'fold' (written vertically), '[?front]' or possibly '[?frog]' (written diagonally), 'this flap only on one' (written diagonally on a central flap), the numeral '9' with a small mark, 'front' and 'bend' (lower portions), and 'x 2' again near the base. A small piece of tape or sticker is visible at the bottom centre. The top right edge shows partial text '3/' which continues on the right page as '3/4 flap only on one'. No handwritten page number is clearly legible — 'P11' appears near the top but may be a pattern/piece reference rather than a diary page number. Cluny is likely Cluny, Burgundy, France.

pages.notes: Page continues the box pattern/template from the left page. Contains a simpler rectangular diagram with labels: '3/4 flap only on one' at the top, small 'a' at left margin and again at bottom left, 'bend' written vertically near centre, 'door' written diagonally in the right section, and 'bend only on one' written diagonally at the lower right. The notebook's printed watermark 'Orla Kiely' and 'All designs are protected by copyright, design right owned by Orla Kiely and D Rowan.' appear at the bottom — these are printed elements of the notebook, not handwritten diary content. The handwritten page number '110' appears top right. No diary narrative or travel content on this spread.
